bird.name: 掘穴鸚鵡
bird.summary: 穴鹦哥（學名：Cyanoliseus patagonus，西班牙語：Loro barranquero，台湾作掘穴鸚鵡），是鸚形目新世界鸚鵡科掘穴鸚鵡屬下的唯一一種現存鳥類。這種鳥類分佈在南美洲的南錐體地區，包含乌拉圭、智利及阿根廷。因其擁有廣大的分佈範圍，且數量減少的趨勢並不嚴…

locations.name: 路易·皮埃尔·维埃约
locations.summary: 路易·皮埃尔·维埃约（法語：Louis Pierre Vieillot，1748年5月10日—1830年8月24日）是法国的鳥類學家。 维埃约是第一批用卡尔·林奈式二名法科学描述鸟类的研究者之一。至少有26个他命名的鸟类至今仍在学术界使用…

locations.name: 阿根廷
locations.summary: 阿根廷共和国（西班牙語：República Argentina），通称阿根廷（西班牙語：Argentina，發音：[aɾxenˈtina] ），是位于南美洲南部的主权国家。由23个省和布宜諾斯艾利斯自治市组成的联邦共和国，占有南錐體的大部…

locations.name: 智利
locations.summary: 智利共和国（西班牙語：República de Chile），通稱智利（西班牙語：Chile），是位於南美洲的国家，智利西部与南部濒临太平洋，北靠秘鲁，东邻玻利维亚及阿根廷。為南美洲國家聯盟的成員國。除此之外，智利与厄瓜多尔相同，是两個不…

locations.name: 大查科
locations.summary: 大查科（西班牙語：Gran Chaco，直译：「大獵場」），又译作大厦谷，是南美洲中部的冲积平原。这个地名是由西班牙语“Gran”（大的）和瓜拉尼语“Chaco”（狩猎场）组成。 概述 大查科位于安第斯山脉以东，巴拉圭河－巴拉那河以西，亚…

relations.subject: 掘穴鸚鵡
relations.evidence: The burrowing parrot (Cyanoliseus patagonus), also known as the burrowing parakeet or the Patagonian conure, is a species of parrot native to Argentina and Chile

relations.subject: 掘穴鸚鵡
relations.evidence: The burrowing parrot can be found in much of Argentina, and there are isolated populations in central Chile. In the winter, birds in central and southern Argentina may migrate north as far as southern Uruguay, making them austral migrants, while Chilean birds migrate vertically down slope to avoid colder altitudes. Movements in the populations of northwestern Argentina are also known to occur according to food availability. The burrowing parrot prefers dry, open country, particularly in the vicinity of water courses, up to 2000 m in elevation. Habitats include montane grassy shrubland, Patagonian steppes, arid lowland, woodland savanna, and the plains of the Gran Chaco. They may also inhabit farmland and the edges of urban areas

relations.subject: 掘穴鸚鵡
relations.evidence: thumb|251x251px|Burrowing parrot in captivity The burrowing parrot currently has an overall conservation status of least concern according to the IUCN Red List, but populations are currently declining, due to exploitation for the wildlife trade and persecution as a crop pest. Their nesting habits make them particularly vulnerable to human disturbances and habitat degradation.
